USNDA praises 'Countering Chinese Drones' provisions in FY 2025 NDAA, urges Congress to support American drone industrial base.
news.summary: On December 9, 2024, in Washington, the final text of the Fiscal Year 2025 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) was released. Nathan Ecelbarger, the spokesperson for the provisional Board of Directors for the United States National Drone Association (USNDA), made a statement in response.

news.headline: Billions flow into Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs as the US dominates the market; Switzerland lags.
news.summary: The United States has solidified its position as the leading market for digital asset investment products, with Switzerland significantly lagging behind in comparison.

news.headline: Lomiko Metals appoints new CEO, Gordana Slepcev, amid Canada Post strike; Belinda Labatte to remain as Executive Chair, CEO, and Interim Board Chair.
news.summary: In Montreal, Lomiko Metals Inc. (TSX.V: LMR) has shared news of a leadership change, with Gordana Slepcev transitioning from Chief Operating Officer to Chief Executive Officer as of December 19, 2024. Belinda Labatte will continue in the role of Executive Chair. The Chief Operating Officer position will not be filled. Belinda Labatte, the current CEO and Interim Chair of the Board, expressed satisfaction with the transition, highlighting shared values and vision for the company's growth.

news.headline: Harrison Street named 'Best Place to Work in Money Management' for 10th year, managing $56B in assets under management.
news.summary: A renowned investment management firm, Harrison Street, based in Chicago, has recently been recognized as one of the top workplaces in the money management sector. With assets totaling around $56 billion under management, Harrison Street specializes in alternative real assets. This recognition highlights their commitment to creating a positive work environment and signifies their esteemed position in the industry.

Imperial Metals and Mount Polley face charges for Fisheries Act violations from 2014. Charges filed in BC court on Dec 6, 2024.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on December 09, 2024, Imperial Metals Corporation (TSX:III) and its subsidiary Mount Polley Mining Corporation, in partnership with engineering firm Wood Canada Limited, are facing charges for purported breaches of the federal Fisheries Act linked to the collapse of the Tailings Storage Facility at the Mount Polley Mine over a decade ago in August 2014. The charges were presented in an official indictment submitted to the Supreme Court of British Columbia on December 6, 2024, as confirmed by the Company today.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate has surged to 6.1%, a significant increase from the previous 4.3% rate. news.headline: CARVYKTI® boosts MRD-negativity rates for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients vs standard care - results from CARTITUDE-4 study presented at ASH expo. (288)
news.summary: Legend Biotech Corporation announced new results from the Phase 3 CARTITUDE-4 study, showing that a single infusion of CARVYKTI® provided higher rates of minimal residual disease (MRD)-negativity in patients with relapsed or lenalidomide-refractory multiple myeloma. The study included patients who received at least one prior line of therapy, including a proteasome inhibitor and an immunomodulatory agent, and compared to standard therapies of pomalidomide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone or daratumumab, pomalidomide, and dexamethasone. The achievement of MRD negativity is associated with prolonged survival outcomes for multiple myeloma patients. The results were presented at the 66th American Society of Hematology Annual Meeting and Exposition, reinforcing the clinical value of CARVYKTI® as early as the second line and supporting the recent achievement of overall survival benefit versus standard therapies. (Words: 126)

news.headline: CARVYKTI® showed 89% MRD negativity vs standard therapies in CARTITUDE-4 study, with most achieving it in <2 months.
news.summary: Among the patients evaluated for minimal residual disease (MRD) assessment, an overwhelming 89 percent tested negative for MRD. A majority of these patients achieved MRD negativity in less than 2 months. These findings contribute to the recently unveiled benefits in overall survival (OS), distinguishing this cell therapy as the first and sole treatment proven to notably prolong OS compared to standard options.

news.headline: Antengene reports results from two late-stage studies of selinexor at ASH 2024. Exciting progress in biopharmaceuticals.
news.summary: On December 9, 2024, Antengene Corporation Limited, a prominent global biopharmaceutical company, announced its dedication to discovering, developing, and commercializing groundbreaking medicines. Headquartered in Shanghai and Hong Kong, Antengene, listed as SEHK: 6996.HK, focuses on bringing first-in-class and/or best-in-class drugs to market.

news.headline: GATE Energy wins commissioning contract for Shell Offshore's Sparta project, covering owner's engineering and construction management.
news.summary: In Houston on December 9, 2024, GATE Energy reveals securing a significant contract from Shell Offshore, Inc. for the Sparta semi-submersible project. The contract encompasses commissioning management and execution, with GATE Energy offering a range of services including owner's engineering and construction management. This achievement marks a positive development for GATE Energy and highlights their strong position in the industry.

news.headline: Keros Therapeutics shares clinical data at ASH meeting on elritercept program targeting TGF-ß proteins in two Phase 2 trials for MDS and MF patients.
news.summary: On December 9, 2024, Keros Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed new information at the 66th American Society of Hematology Annual Meeting and Exposition. The company, listed as KROS on Nasdaq, shared additional data from two ongoing Phase 2 clinical trials involving elritercept (KER-050). One trial focuses on patients with very low-, low-, or intermediate-risk myelodysplastic syndromes (MDS), while the other targets patients with myelofibrosis (MF). The data was presented both in person in San Diego, California, and virtually from December 7-10, 2024. Keros is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical firm specializing in developing and marketing innovative treatments for patients with conditions associated with abnormal signaling of the transforming growth factor-beta (TGF-ß) family of proteins.

news.headline: Autolus Therapeutics updates clinical data at ASH Annual Meeting 2024.
news.summary: Autolus Therapeutics recently shared key clinical data updates at the prestigious American Society of Hematology (ASH) Annual Meeting held in 2024. The company highlighted significant findings from their research, showcasing key figures and results that indicate promising advancements in the field of hematology. This presentation at ASH, a renowned event in the medical community, serves to underscore Autolus Therapeutics' commitment to innovation and the development of potentially impactful treatments. The data shared at the meeting, including crucial numbers and outcomes, is a testament to the company's dedication to advancing medical science in the fight against hematologic disorders.

news.headline: New AI16z DAO w/ AI tech plans to disrupt Silicon Valley w/ data-driven financial decisions for crypto communities.
news.summary: The AI16z DAO is revolutionizing the way crypto communities invest, govern, and operate by utilizing AI technology for making data-driven financial decisions. This initiative marks a significant shift in the cryptocurrency space, promising to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of community-led projects. With this approach, AI16z DAO aims to streamline processes, promote transparency, and maximize returns for its stakeholders. By tapping into the power of AI, the DAO is poised to disrupt traditional models of decision-making, potentially attracting a broader base of participants and investors.

news.headline: SSC Security Services Corp. reports FY2024 results: 11% revenue growth to $120.6M, 18% EBITDA/share growth, buys back 3% shares.
news.summary: SSC Security Services Corp. released its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ended September 30, 2024. The company reported a revenue of $120.6 million for the full fiscal year, representing an 11% increase from the previous year. In Q4, revenue was $29.6 million, up 1% from the same quarter last year. The adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year was $5.0 million ($0.26 per share), an 18% increase from the prior year. Cash and cash equivalents stood at $13.3 million, loans and mortgages receivable at $1.0 million, and total shareholders' equity at $64.5 million with no long-term debt. Dividends of $0.12 per share were paid, and 591,400 shares were bought back at an average of $2.52 per share.

news.headline: ThreeD Capital Inc. reports unaudited November 30, 2024 Net Asset Value Per Share of $0.77. (#s CSE:IDK, OTCQX:IDKFF)
news.summary: On December 9, 2024, a Canadian venture capital firm, ThreeD Capital Inc., disclosed that as of November 30, 2024, its unaudited net asset value per share stood at $0.77. The company, trading as CSE:IDK and OTCQX:IDKFF, specializes in making strategic investments in firms operating within the junior resources and disruptive technologies industries. This figure indicates the value attributed to each share owned by the firm at that specific date, showcasing its financial standing within the market.

news.headline: Bitmain, a Bitcoin ASIC maker, expands production to US amidst delays in deliveries of ASICs stuck at ports for weeks.
news.summary: US Bitcoin miners are currently facing challenges due to delayed deliveries of Bitmain ASICs that have been held up at ports of entry in the United States for several weeks. This has resulted in significant disruptions for miners, impacting their ability to operate efficiently and affecting the overall Bitcoin mining industry. The exact number of ASICs held up at US ports has not been disclosed, but reports suggest a substantial number of deliveries are affected. This delay has highlighted the logistical issues faced by Bitcoin miners in the US and the importance of timely delivery of mining equipment for their operations.

AMCON Distributing Co. (NYSE: DIT) acquires Arrowrock Supply from Boise. CEO expresses excitement. #businessgrows
news.summary: Omaha, Nebraska-based consumer products company, AMCON Distributing Company ("AMCON"), recently acquired Arrowrock Supply of Boise, Idaho, from Davis-Jones Inc. The acquisition included welcoming Arrowrock's employees, customers, and vendors to the AMCON Family. AMCON's Chairman and CEO, Christopher H. Atayan, expressed delight in the acquisition. The company, listed on NYSE American as DIT, operates in the Intermountain West region of the United States.

Dave & Buster's (NASDAQ: PLAY) Q3 results to be announced; Q2 earnings exceeded but revenues missed with a 6.3% sales decline. CEO optimistic about strategic goals and remodeled stores' potential.
news.summary: Dave & Buster’s Entertainment, Inc. (NASDAQ:PLAY) is scheduled to announce its third-quarter financial results after the closing bell on Tuesday. In the previous quarter, the company surpassed earnings forecasts but fell short on revenue expectations due to a 6.3% decline in comparable sales. CEO Chris Morris highlighted advancements in strategic objectives and store renovations as potential drivers for upcoming quarters. The CEO remains optimistic about the fully programmed remodels of stores. For further details on this news story, refer to a complete article on Benzinga.com.

BitRail to present at New England Venture Summit on Dec. 10-11, 2024 in Boston. Organized by youngStartup Ventures.

The event, hosted by youngStartup Ventures, will provide a platform for networking and features innovative companies like BitRail.

news.headline: Quobly achieves milestone using FD-SOI for fault-tolerant quantum computing, potential scalability for commercial use.
news.summary: A French quantum computing startup, Quobly, announced that FD-SOI technology can be a scalable platform for commercial quantum computing. This technology allows leveraging traditional semiconductor manufacturing fabs. The announcement was made in Grenoble, France and San Francisco on December 9, 2024.

news.headline: Cassius Ventures Ltd. addresses mailing delays due to Canada Post strike; Annual general meeting to be held on Dec. 30, 2024 at 595 Burrard Street, Vancouver. Shareholders may not receive physical copies of Meeting Materials on time.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, Cassius Ventures Ltd. announces that the delivery of Meeting Materials for their upcoming annual general meeting at 595 Burrard Street, Suite 3083, Vancouver on December 30, 2024 at 11 a.m. (PST) will be delayed due to the Canada Post postal strike. Shareholders might not receive physical copies of the Meeting Materials before the Meeting until the strike is resolved. This strike's impact could affect the timely distribution of important documents to shareholders, potentially leading to complications in participation.

news.headline: Michael Saylor used sports analogy to explain Bitcoin to Dave Portnoy; Satoshi provided game where all can win.
news.summary: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy sought clarification on Bitcoin from MicroStrategy Chairman Michael Saylor. Portnoy had sold his Bitcoin after feeling confused by the Winklevoss twins in 2020. Saylor, a prominent figure in Bitcoin education and holding a significant amount of the cryptocurrency due to MicroStrategy's strategy, used sports analogies to explain Bitcoin to Portnoy. He described Bitcoin as a game that is not rigged against the public, unlike some real-world scenarios like sports ticket prices and casino games that favor the house. Saylor aimed to clarify any misunderstandings Portnoy had about Bitcoin during the interview. This interaction can be viewed as an attempt to provide better understanding of Bitcoin to a wider audience, particularly those interested but confused by the complexities of the cryptocurrency.

news.headline: AKA Identity offers early access to AI-native platform for identity management due to high demand. Founded by William Lin and Rob Fry.
news.summary: A new company called AKA Identity has introduced its innovative platform early access program in San Francisco today. This launch follows the overwhelming response to its workforce identity baseline assessment program, which was subscribed by multiple enterprise clients in various industries such as education, energy, financial services, healthcare, retail, and technology. The company was founded by cybersecurity experts William Lin, serving as CEO, and Rob Fry, as CTO.
